transcript: average of the 30 largest stocks on the New York Stock Exchange. These 30 stocks are so heavily traded that they represent the majority of the market volume. Therefore, these 30 stocks are generally a good indicator of the entire market. The S&P index takes a weighted average of the 500 most widely held stocks on the market. These 500 stocks account for approximately 70% of the entire market. Most professionals use the S&P's performance as their benchmark. Lastly, the NASDAQ looks at over 3,000 stocks. The NASDAQ used to be an index exclusively for tech stocks but has grown to include a few stocks from different industries as well. You can learn more about indices by checking out WallStreetSurvivor.com
